km.Hulse, Cheshire, England: ហូលសិ, ឆេសហីរេ, ប្រទេសអង់គ្លេស

zh.lifeSummary: David de Hulse於1265年出生在英國爾赫爾斯。他和Enid至少有1個兒子。他去世於1305年在他的家鄉享年40歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: David de Hulse于1265年出生在英国爾赫爾斯。他和Enid至少有1个儿子。他去世于1305年在他的家乡享年40岁。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Hulse
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (Cheshire and Staffordshire): habitational name from Hulse in Witton (Cheshire), from Middle English holes, plural of hole ‘hole, hollow, depression’ (Old English holh). See Hole .
North German (Hülse): topographic name for someone who lived where holly grew, Middle Low German huls, hüls.
American shortened and altered form of Flemish Holsaert (see Hulsart ). Compare Hulce , Huls , and Hults 1.

variants.name: Hulsart
meanings: Americanized form of Flemish Holsaert: unexplained. See also Hulse 3.

variants.name: Hole
meanings: English (southwestern): topographic name for someone who lived in or by a hole or hollow, from Middle English hol(e) (Old English hol, holh, dative hole), or a habitational name from some minor place so named.
Norwegian: habitational name from any of numerous farmsteads, so named from the dative singular or indefinite plural form of Old Norse hóll ‘round hill, mound’.

In some cases possibly also an American shortened form of Flemish Van Hole: habitational name from the common placename Hol(e), meaning ‘hollow, valley’; or a topographic name from the same term or from Middle Dutch hole, heule ‘(low) arched bridge, weir’.

variants.name: Hults
meanings: Altered form of   Hulse  , a surname of Flemish (and also of English and German) origin. Compare   Hultz   1.
In some cases also a variant of   Hultz   2, a surname of German origin.

variants.name: Hulce
meanings: Altered form of   Hulse  , a surname of Flemish (and also of English and German) origin.

nameMeanings.name: David
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Biblical name, borne by the greatest of all the kings of Israel, whose history is recounted with great vividness in the first and second books of Samuel and elsewhere. As a boy he killed the giant Philistine Goliath with his slingshot. As king of Judah, and later of all Israel, he expanded the power of the Israelites and established the security of their kingdom. He was also noted as a poet, many of the Psalms being attributed to him. The Hebrew derivation of the name is uncertain; it is said by some to represent a nursery word meaning ‘darling’. It is a very popular Jewish name, but is almost equally common among Gentiles in the English-speaking world. It is particularly common in Wales and Scotland, having been borne by the patron saint of Wales (  see  Dewi   ) and by two medieval kings of Scotland.

historicEvents.title: Jews Expelled From England
historicEvents.description: After years of anti-Semitic behavior, King Edward I expelled Jews from England in 1290.

en.lifeSummary: David de Hulse was born in 1265, in Hulse, Cheshire, England. He had at least 1 son with Enid. He died in 1305, in his hometown, at the age of 40.

fr.lifeSummary: David de Hulse est né en 1265, à Hulse, Cheshire, Angleterre. Il a eu au moins 1 fils avec Enid. Il est décédé en 1305, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 40 ans.

de.lifeSummary: David de Hulse wurde 1265 in Hulse, Cheshire, England geboren. Er hatte mit Enid mindestens 1 Sohn. Er starb 1305 im Alter von 40 Jahren in seinem Geburtsort.

it.lifeSummary: David de Hulse è nato il nel 1265 nel Hulse, Cheshire, England. Lui avuto almeno 1 figlio con Enid. Morì nel 1305, nel sua cittadina, all'età di 40 anni.

ja.Hulse, Cheshire, England: イギリスチェシュアイアハルゼ
ja.lifeSummary: David de Hulseは1265年にイギリスチェシュアイアハルゼで生まれた。彼はEnidとの間に少なくとも1人の息子をもうけた。彼は1305年に故郷で40歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: David de Hulse(은)는 1265년 영국 체실르 훌스에서 태어났다. 그는 Enid(와)과 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 1305년 그의 고향에서 40세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: David de Hulse nasceu em 1265, em Hulse, Cheshire, Inglaterra. Ele teve pelo menos 1 filho com Enid. Ele faleceu em 1305, em sua cidade natal, com 40 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: David de Hulse родился в 1265 г. в местности хулс, чешир, Англия. У него был по крайней мере 1 сын от Enid. Он умер в 1305 г. в его родном городе в возрасте 40 лет.

es.lifeSummary: David de Hulse nació en 1265, en Hulse, Cheshire, Inglaterra. Tuvo por lo menos 1 hijo con Enid. Murió en 1305, en su pueblo, a la edad de 40 años.
